settings.title: Our Free Legal Clinic Turns Ten
settings.excerpt: A decade of monthly drop-in advice has now helped more than two thousand local residents.
settings.body:
Ten years ago a few of us started staying late one evening a month to answer legal questions for anyone who walked in, free of charge. We were not sure many people would come.

Since then the Greystone community clinic has helped more than two thousand people with everything from tenancy worries to consumer disputes. Most needed nothing more than clear information and a sense of which way to turn.

What ten years has taught us

The lesson that has stayed with us is how often a problem feels enormous only because nobody has explained it plainly. A tenant facing eviction or a customer fighting a faulty purchase often arrives expecting the worst, and leaves simply knowing what their rights are and what step comes next.

Over the years the questions have fallen into a few familiar groups.

  • Renting, deposits, and disputes with landlords
  • Faulty goods and unfair charges
  • Money owed and small claims
  • Family matters and where to find proper support

What stays the same

The clinic still runs on the first Tuesday of every month, and it always will. You do not need an appointment, and you will not be asked to become a client. The point has never been to win business. It is to make sure good advice is not something only certain people can afford.

Thank you to the volunteers who have given up their evenings to keep it going for a decade. They are the reason a quiet idea has turned into two thousand people helped, and counting.
